meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Finish Your First App
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to master AI-assisted debugging using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first real-world software project today.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, stop debugging in isolation. Use AI to act as your pair programmer, transforming error logs into actionable code fixes. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you turn blockers into learning milestones, ensuring every project reaches a deployable state.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and silent, unresolved syntax errors. You start with a vision of a polished app, but somewhere between the third broken API call and a cryptic stack trace, the momentum dies. It is not a lack of talent; it is a lack of a systematic way to bridge the gap between 'code broken' and 'feature working.'

There is a better way. Modern engineering is no longer about memorizing syntax; it is about orchestrating AI to solve the friction points that stall your progress. By leveraging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can turn your development environment into a high-velocity lab, where every bug is just a prompt away from resolution.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope of the failure. Avoid the temptation to refactor the whole file; focus only on the breaking function.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file and identify the specific logic block causing the runtime exception. Explain the root cause in one sentence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'identify the specific logic block causing the error in index.ts'

steps.title: Contextualize the Error
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and the relevant code snippet to the AI to provide a clear picture of the state mismatch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am receiving this error [INSERT ERROR]. Here is the relevant function code. Propose a fix that maintains my existing design pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/app.ts --task 'fix the type mismatch in the user-auth function based on the error log'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Apply the fix in small, testable increments to ensure you don't introduce regression bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the minimal code diff required to resolve the bug while maintaining state persistence.

steps.title: Automated Verification
steps.description: Use AI to write a unit test that confirms the bug is squashed and will not reappear in future builds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that specifically triggers the scenario that caused the previous crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate a unit test for the fixed function and run it'

steps.title: Documentation of Logic
steps.description: Comment your code to explain 'why' a specific fix was implemented, ensuring you understand the logic for your portfolio review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add JSDoc comments to this function explaining the logic flow and the bug fix implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add JSDoc comments to the modified function explaining the fix'

pro_tips: Treat AI prompts as 'specs'—the more precise your constraints, the higher the quality of the generated fix.
Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix; if you don't understand the solution, you haven't really finished the project.
Use version control branches for every AI-suggested change; if the AI hallucinates, you can revert in seconds.
Limit your AI context window to the specific module you are working on to prevent 'context bloat' and reduced accuracy.
When a bug persists, ask the AI to 'act as a senior reviewer' and audit your entire file for anti-patterns, not just the error.
content.next_steps: Once your project runs without errors, push your code to GitHub, write a brief README explaining the technical hurdles you overcame, and start your next feature module.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to move from 'stuck' to 'shipped' every single time. Stop letting bugs define your limits and start building the portfolio that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
